_comment_enabled: Set to true to actually POST to webhook_url. When false, pager.py classifies events but does not deliver — undelivered.jsonl stays empty.
_comment_webhook_url: Generic JSON-POST endpoint. Tested shapes: PagerDuty Events API v2, Opsgenie webhook, Slack incoming webhook (will need a transformer for Slack's expected shape — pager posts the generic event payload).
_comment_min_severity: One of DEBUG | INFO | LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH | CRITICAL. Pages fire when audit row severity >= this floor. Default HIGH per F-011 audit finding.
_comment_rate_limit_per_min: Token bucket per minute. Excess events are SUPPRESSED (not enqueued to undelivered) — suppression is intentional, not a delivery failure.
_comment_max_retries: After this many retry attempts on an undelivered event, the entry is dropped from paging-undelivered.jsonl and logged as exhausted.
_setup: 1. Copy this file to paging-config.json (gitignored — contains your webhook URL).
2. Set enabled=true, paste your webhook URL.
3. Pick min_severity. HIGH means HIGH and CRITICAL trigger; CRITICAL means only CRITICAL.
4. Wire pager.py into a periodic runner (cron, systemd timer, audit hook). Suggested cadence: every 30s during business hours, every 5m off-hours.
5. Smoke-test: run pager.py --dry-run after seeding a HIGH-severity row in audit.jsonl.
